reasoning: The market price on October 30, 2025, was 3%, reflecting the extreme unlikelihood of a new coronavirus pandemic being declared by the WHO before December 31, 2025. Historically, new pandemics are very rare (base rate << 1% per year), and the WHO is notoriously slow in officially declaring them (often taking months after a widespread outbreak begins). Given the knowledge cutoff date in early November 2025, even if an outbreak were to start immediately, it is highly improbable the WHO would formulate an official 'pandemic' declaration within the remaining seven weeks of the year. I am forecasting the minimum allowed boundary of 2%.
